name: future-bright
description: Michael E. Martinez's 'Future Bright: A Transforming Vision of Human Intelligence' — a comprehensive exploration of intelligence as a learnable, modifiable human capacity. Covers what intelligence is, the varieties of intelligence, brain and mind, the nature-nurture debate, how experience cultivates intelligence, the superstructure of effectiveness, and 10 specific strategies to enhance intelligence.
useCase: Understanding that intelligence is not fixed but can be enhanced through experience, learning the three components of intelligence (fluid, crystallized, effective character), discovering how the brain and mind enable intelligence, and applying strategies to boost your own and others' intelligence.
knownRisks: Academic writing style — may feel dense for casual readers
Published in 2013 — some neuroscience may be slightly dated
Author passed away before publication — the book was completed by colleagues
Some claims about the malleability of intelligence are still debated
